attacking_style: Pure sprinter who relies on positioning and power rather than explosive kick. Finishes strongest on technical surfaces (cobbles, bumpy finishes) and long straights (1300m+) where raw power trumps acceleration.
team_tactics: Leadout execution has been consistently problematic across teams (UNO-X, UAE). Often forced to wheel-surf rivals (Girmay, others) rather than rely on own train. Peaks too early (400m out) leaving him vulnerable to better-timed rivals.
terrain_preference: Excels on technical surfaces (cobbles, bumpy Champs-Élysées), tailwind power finishes, and flat sprints. Completely eliminated by even Cat 3/4 climbs or selective circuit racing with any vertical component.
vulnerability: Cannot survive categorized climbs or selective hilltop finishes (dropped repeatedly 2019-2022). Lacks final 50m acceleration against top-tier sprinters. Poor bike-throw technique costs close finishes. Loses organizational cohesion in technical sprint positioning.
key_patterns: Repeatedly dropped on Cat 3/4 climbs and circuit racing; pure sprinter with zero climbing residual (5+ instances)
Positioned third-wheel consistently but lacks final 50m kick to win against elite sprinters (UAE Tour pattern)
Technical surface specialist: won cobbled Champs-Élysées, bumpy finishes where handling separates equals (3 instances)
Course knowledge decisive advantage: exploited prior Oman wins for perfect headwind positioning (2 documented wins)
Leadout coordination failures across multiple teams; peaks 400m out, vulnerable to better-timed rivals (4+ races)
Form decline trajectory 2021-2023: positioned well but legs fail in final 200m execution
form_trajectory: negative: Clear decline 2021-2023 with repeated instances of good positioning but failed execution in final meters, plus inability to survive even minor climbing that previously managed
